title: Quality of life improvements for managing workflow state
body:
## In what area(s)?

/area runtime
/area scheduler

## Describe the feature

Right now, Dapr only concerns itself with workflow state that it's actively managing.  Once a workflow is in any finished state, maintenance and cleanup is currently seen as an outside concern.
_(There is one exception in that we do offer the advice that users can configure a TTL on the store backing their workflows.  Unfortunately, this is outside the scheduler lifecycle and can result in a [foot gun](https://github.com/dapr/dapr/issues/9018) as there's nothing connecting the two lifecycles.)_

There are improvements we can make to Dapr workflows that could help make users' experiences feel more guided and structured.  The specific scenarios worth tackling are:

1. Scheduler-level configurable TTLs for workflows
2. Explicitly configurable success and DLQ destinations

**Important:** I suspect offering any kind of API for explicit cleanup operations that users would have to integrate with won't be a "great experience"™️ and that this functionality is ideally accomplished as part of configuring Dapr components.

### 1 - Scheduler-level TTLs
This would just be a more explicit implementation at the scheduler level, rather than relying on the backing store to provide expiry semantics. The benefit being that we wouldn't be encouraging users to configure the system to pull the rug out from under itself. 😜 

### 2 - Explicit success and DLQ destinations
This could come in the form of some additional configuration options for workflows where they could define a different backing store for the workflow engine to move jobs to.  One for success, one for failed.

## Release Note
<!-- How should this new feature be announced in our release notes? It can be populated later. -->
<!-- Keep it as a single line. Examples: -->

<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **ADD** New feature in Dapr. -->
<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **FIX** Bug in runtime. -->
<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **UPDATE** Runtime dependency. -->

RELEASE NOTE:

comments.body: A prerequisite to this is the completion of the [Workflows Lifecycle events](https://github.com/dapr/dapr/issues/6072) feature, which is being delivered by @cicoyle via the [Event Stream](https://github.com/dapr/proposals/pull/77) proposal, which was originally targeted for the 1.16 release time frame, but has been deferred to a future release.
